Llama-3.1-70B.joke_text: Why did my wife go to the beauty parlor? To get a paws-itively gorgeous haircut.

Qwen3-30B-A3B.joke_text: I told my comedian friend he should try being serious for once. He said, “I am serious—I’m just serious about being funny.”
I asked Jesus how to handle my existential dread. He said, “Just remember: I’ve been through worse—like walking on water and getting crucified.”
I told my dog a pun about books, but he just gave me a *paw*-sitive look.
I offered my friend a snack. He said, “Punchline,” then vanished into thin air.
I thought I’d finally figured out life when I found a chocolate shaped like a lightbulb… then I bit into it and it wasn’t edible.
I told my child, “You’re not a baby anymore.” They responded, “No, but I still get free food.”
My bed says I’m too dramatic when I say I’m “on the edge”—it’s just a mattress, not a cliff!
My jeans started writing poetry about my digestive trauma. One line read: “I once held your secrets, now I hold your sins.”
My hand started a podcast called *The Grip of Life*—it’s all about emotional support and grip strength.
The hippo waved his Zippo like a conductor’s baton. “This,” he said, “is the difference between chaos and music.”
I offered my hand a promotion, but it declined—said it wasn’t ready to “get a grip” on responsibility.
The difference between a ninja and a lawyer? One hides in shadows, the other hides in paperwork.
I tried to give a eulogy, but I kept getting distracted by how well the casket matched the carpet.
I asked my bed if it believed in love at first sight, and it whispered, “I’ve seen you fall asleep mid-sentence—yes.”
My bed once caught me lying—it said, “I can feel your guilt, you’re not even on the right side!”
I once stole a word from a dictionary and got banned from the library. They said I “used it improperly.”
I told my parent I wanted to be an astronaut. They said, “Good luck with that—your last mission was finding the remote.”
My Alzheimer’s roommate keeps saying he’s never been to Paris. I remind him he’s lived there for 20 years. He says, “Exactly! I must have forgotten.”
I used to think the letter “E” was boring until I realized it’s the most *common* letter… like me at family reunions.
I told my toaster a joke. It popped out and said, “Punchline.”
The judge asked the offender if he had anything to say. He replied, “I just wanted to see how many times I could say ‘offender’ before being arrested.”
My pirate neighbor yells “Arrr!” every time he sees a seagull. I finally said, “You’re not fooling anyone—you’re just mad they don’t bring you snacks.”
My date brought flowers, but I was disappointed—they were just daisies. I told them, “I wanted something with more *date*-ing potential.”
I asked my cow why she never gets nervous before big events—she said, “Because I’ve already survived a thunderstorm, a goat, and a bad haircut.”
I tried to joke with my dictionary. It looked up “Punchline” and said, “That’s not even a word.”
I brought a balloon to the funeral. The priest said, “That’s not appropriate.” I said, “It’s not a balloon—it’s a metaphor for my unresolved emotions.”
I told my crush I loved her like the alphabet loves *O*. She said, “That’s sweet… but I’m not even a vowel.”
I tried to live by the principle that life is like a box of chocolates. But every time I picked one, it turned out to be “Unexpected Loneliness” and “Slightly Melting Guilt.”
My hand formed a band called *The Fingers*, but they only play in minor keys—because they’re always feeling down.
My pirate roommate says he’s allergic to rules. That’s why he’s always breaking them—especially the ones about not stealing other people’s snacks.
I asked my pencil what it dreams about. It whispered, “Being *unwritten*.”
I told my dog he was an offender, and now he won’t stop barking at the mailman—turns out he’s got a restraining order against him.
My penis once got stuck in a vending machine. It wasn’t for snacks—it was for self-actualization.
The eulogy was so long, I fell asleep and dreamt I was the one being buried. When I woke up, I realized I was already dead… to fashion sense.
I told my vegan brother he should try meat. He replied, “I’d rather try *not* being a murderer.”
My Alzheimer’s uncle tried to pay for groceries with a coupon from 1987. The cashier said, “Sir, this expired 30 years ago.” He said, “Exactly! That’s why it’s valuable.”
I challenged the pirate to a spelling bee. He won by yelling “ARRR” at every word.
The letter “M” started a podcast called “Matter of Fact”—but it’s just “M” talking about “M” for two hours straight.
My hair doesn’t just grow—it *plots*.
My pizza is a terrible listener—it always cuts me off.
The hippo tried to light a cigar with his Zippo, but it turned into a tiny rubber duck. “Ah,” he sighed, “the difference between expectation and reality.”
I resolved to quit social media. I lasted three days. Then I posted a photo of my resolution failure.
My therapist says I have a preoccupation with negative thoughts. I told her, “That’s just my positive side.”
My hair has a better attention span than my dog.
I broke up with my girlfriend because she was too *C*-rude. Now I’m dating someone who’s all *U*—like, “You’re so *U*!”
The man sat on the chair and said, “I feel invisible.” The table replied, “Not really—I’ve been staring at you since you walked in.”
My dad says I’m too emotional. I said, “Dad, I’m just passionate. Unlike you, who cried during *The Lion King*… but only because he didn’t get the job.”
After the funeral, I told my mom, “I think Dad’s in a better place.” She replied, “He’s in the basement. We’re still arguing about the will.”
The hippo tried to use his Zippo to light a match, but the match said, “Dude, we’re not even in the same dimension.”
I named my boomerang “Regret.” It hasn’t come back since I said that out loud.

deepseek-chat.joke_text: I took a DNA test and it revealed a strong genetic predisposition for dramatic exits.
The scarecrow's performance review was rough; they said he was outstanding in his field, but lacked drive.
The tiger complained to its tailor, "This stripe is itchy," and the tailor said, "Well, that's just how you spot a rash decision."

I wore a shirt made of watches—it was a time-consuming fashion choice.

They were the perfect couple: one loved giving unsolicited advice, and the other loved not taking it.

A neutron orders a drink and the bartender replies, "Are you positive you're not going to cause a reaction?"
A father's advice: "Never follow anyone's footsteps... unless they're leaving a bakery."
My dentist said I have a cavity, but I told him it's just a bear tooth I'm storing for the winter.
My garbanzo bean got a new suit; now it's a fancy-pants-o bean.
My fridge is a terrible running partner—it always stops for a cool down.
The scarecrow’s memoir, "No Brain, All Gain: My Life in the Field," is favored to win a Nobel.
My romantic candlelit dinner was ruined when the candles started arguing over whose wax was dripping more dramatically.
I tried to arm-wrestle my own leg to settle an argument, but it was a real stand-off.
During the big race, the snail suddenly stopped to admire a flower—guess he was just in it for the shell of it.
I tried to start a habit of mindfulness, but the nun kept offering to sell me cookies instead.
I told my mechanic I had a dream my muffler fell off, and he said, "Sounds like a silent night."
The shortest word in the dictionary is "I," which explains a lot about people.
The cow with a sore foot stood in the field and declared, "I'm having a real hoof-and-mouth disease."
I told my foot a secret, but it went and tattletaled to the ankle.
A dollar walked into a gender reveal party and said, "I'm here to clear up the change."
My friend calls his strict roommate "North Korea" because he forbids snacks after midnight and demands morning calisthenics.
The only thing moving in Congress is the opposite of progress: a con artist's lips.
My plants seem perkier on Fridays. I think they can sense my reduced desire to water them with coffee.
Our family reunion is just a sophisticated game of "Who's most likely to need bail?"
I asked my weed to help me write a joke, but it just kept saying, "Wait, I had a thought... what was I saying?"
My car's alarm is so sensitive, a woman sighing heavily two houses away sets it off. During the hurricane, it just applauded.
The plumber asked the chemist, "What's the difference between your work and mine?" The chemist said, "Well, if I make a mistake, it's a bang. If you make a mistake, it's a stank."
I tried to teach my dog basketball, but he only ever does a dribble.
The grammar pun contest ended in a draw, because nobody could comma to a conclusion.
The racecar driver developed a fear of speed bumps after mistaking one for a launch ramp.
I asked my nose to dance, but it just boogied on down and demanded a tissue.
My habit of leaving dishes in the sink finally made me a saint—specifically, Saint Procrastinata, patron nun of "I'll do it later."
The sheet told me it felt two-dimensional, so I gave it some pillow talk.
The "Slow: Dad Working" sign is for when he's trying to assemble flat-pack furniture in the driveway.
I told my career coach I'm exploring new opportunities. She said, "Your browser history says you're exploring new episodes."

gemini-2.5-flash.joke_text: Why did the drug dealer get a job at the shoe factory? He heard they had a lot of 'soles' to move.

"The **difference** is," the **dealer** told the **hooker**, "my **crack** is so good, it's practically a new **drug** that will make you forget you ever asked."
The *dealer* got busted trying to smuggle a *pair* of *shoes* filled with illicit *drug* capsules across the border.

My doctor told me to exercise more. I said, "Does running late count?" He said, "No, but sex does." I said, "Now you're just being encouraging."
Why don't potatoes like to argue? Because they're always trying to hash things out.
I'd give my last cent to charity, but I wouldn't accept a cent to attend a Nickelback concert.

A man told a woman, "I love you more than pizza!" She immediately knew he was lying.
"This discovery marks a new period in our understanding," beamed the archaeologist, holding up a small, cottony object. "Yes, it's the earliest known tampon," his grant officer confirmed.
My new hobby during the coronavirus? Staring blankly at a wall. It's surprisingly therapeutic.
Why was the man barred from the bar? He tried to pay with Monopoly money.
An archaeologist, known for his dry wit, found an ancient tampon. "Well," he remarked, "that puts an end to *that* period."
Why do *politicians* always blame the previous administration? It's their *reason* for not changing their own *diaper*.
I tried to go on a date during the coronavirus. We just waved at each other from across the street.
My friend said he found the perfect girl online. I asked, "Is she real?" He said, "She has 10,000 followers, she must be!"
What do you call a very polite minute? A "moment of silence."
I'm not avoiding work, I'm just giving it time to appreciate my absence. It's a form of gentle, thoughtful procrastination.
I tried to write a novel, but it felt like literary suicide.
My **dad**'s favorite exercise is a cross between a lunge and a crunch. He calls it lunch.
A philosophical man walks into a bar and asks, "Is this bar truly here, or is it merely a construct of my perception?" The barman said, "Your tab is real, though."
Why don't hormones ever get bored? There's always *something to regulate*.
A man told a woman he treated her like a goddess. She replied, "So you ignore me for months and then expect a miracle?"
A man asks a woman, "What's the best way to remember your wedding anniversary?" She says, "Just forget it once."

gemini-2.5-flash-lite.joke_text: My wife's cooking is so good, it's right up there with cardboard.

My printer jammed. It was a fraction of a disaster, with a denominator of paper cuts.

The detective’s sting operation almost failed when his undercover pussy got distracted by a laser pointer and made a crucial slip.

My wife's cooking is so bland, the only thing right about it is that it's edible.
My wife told me to embrace my mistakes. I'm considering hugging the entire kitchen after I bake.

My therapist said to explore new sensations. So, I bought a banana and a vibrator.

The denominator is the silent partner, the numerator is the star.

My jokes are like a black hole – once you get sucked in, there’s no escaping the laughter. Or the gravity.
My **brain** is a philosopher, constantly questioning the meaning of lint.
The snail’s journey was a testament to the unwavering **direction** of "slow."

My wife's memory is so selective, she only remembers the right things.
Why did the right woman bring a magnifying glass to the concert? She wanted to see the right performer.
A mafia hitman accidentally brought his pussy to a job, and it kept trying to slip into the victim's pockets for treats.
I told my wife she was wrong. She said, "That's not right!" I said, "Exactly."
The baker’s therapist told him to express himself. So he sculpted a magnificent poo out of marzipan with his bare hand.
My chemistry teacher told me Argon was noble. I guess it's above all that reactive drama.
A man tried to impress a date by making fancy coffee. He ended up with a latte that looked like a Rorschach test.
I’m not procrastinating, I’m just decompressing from all the work I haven't done.
The mafia’s secret tunnels were discovered when a curious pussy decided to slip into one and got lost.
The hitman’s pussy was a master of stealth, able to slip into secure locations and leave a single, incriminating strand of fur.
A man walks into a bar carrying a giant rubber chicken. He orders a beer. The bartender asks, "What's with the chicken?" The man replies, "I'm practicing my mime act, but it keeps pecking me. Ouch!"
What do you call a lazy man who loves coffee? A bean-counter.

gemini-2.5-pro.joke_text: This girl's name was "Deja," and her number was... wait, haven't I gotten this girl's name and number before?
What's the difference between my wife's favorite dish and our sex life? She actually finishes her favorite dish.
My daughter Anna, the drummer, has two volume settings: loud and "Is the building structurally sound?"
My friend told a girl he was a "man of mystery." Her friend found him struggling to open a jar of pickles for 20 minutes.
I asked a girl for her name and number. She said, "My name is Alexa, and my number is one, the loneliest number."
My daughter Anna, the drummer, says the best part of being in a band is getting to sit down the whole time.
The three words my wife dreads most are "We need to talk." The three words I dread most are "The sex dish." It's a casserole she makes when she's mad at me.
The main difference between my singing voice and a grand piano is that one is a beautiful instrument and the other sounds like I'm gargling tuna.
A German engineer designed a lightbulb that lasts 200 years, but you need a specific government form to turn it on.
Why is Yoda so calm? Because try, he does not. Do, he does.
What do you call a nut that solves crimes? Sher-lock Holmes. No, that's not right. A private eye-mond.
My daughter Anna, the drummer, says she can solve any argument with a well-timed cymbal crash. It’s not effective, but it is final.
How do you make a bandstand? You take away their chairs.
My pop saw a weasel try to use a coupon at a bar. The bartender said, "This is expired." The weasel replied, "Can't weasel a deal here, huh?"
I told my wife I'd do any dish she wanted if we had sex. She handed me a Brillo pad and pointed to the sink.
I asked a girl for her name and number. She said, "My name is unimportant, and my number is up." Then she jumped out of the plane. She was my skydiving instructor.
Yoda's favorite nursery rhyme is "Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star... as it looked 800 years ago."
The difference between a politician and a Goodyear tire is the tire only gets inflated once a year. It also offers more protection than a flimsy condom.
What do you call a bar run by a weasel's father? A Pop-up bar.
I tried to invest in the nut market, but it was too volatile. The prices were always going up and down in a nutshell.
I have a new business idea: a can of mixed nuts where they're all just your favorite one. I'll call it "The Only Nut You'll Ever Need."
The family dog has learned to fetch earplugs for Anna whenever her daughter, the drummer, picks up her sticks.
I bought a self-help book called "Unleash Your Inner Nut." I'm still on chapter one: "Finding Your Shell."
The chicken's favorite game: Knock-Knock-Ditch. The idiot's house was right across the road.
The first rule of the Procrastinator's Forum is... actually, the mod will get around to writing it eventually.
The road was blocked by a chicken playing chess with an idiot on the porch of a tiny house. The chicken was winning.
My fish has an inferiority complex, so I bought him a little plastic gun for his tank. Now he has a superiority complex and a list of demands.
A weasel walks into a bar and shouts, "Bartender! A pop on the rocks!" The bartender serves it, and the weasel immediately smashes the bottle on a stone he brought from his pocket.
The attire makes all the difference: a man in a suit on a bicycle is eco-conscious; a man in a suit on a unicycle is late for clown court.
The bartender noticed the weasel was crying. "What's wrong?" he asked. The weasel sobbed, "I just heard a sad pop song about a ferret who lost his keys. It really hit home."
Why was the horse so upset at the barbecue? He overheard someone say, "This coleslaw needs more than a neigh-sayer, it needs more mayo."
How does my daughter Anna, the drummer, start a letter? "Dear Mom & Dad, one, two, one, two, three, four..."
I downloaded a realism mod for my cooking game. Now I just get third-degree burns and cry over a ruined dinner.
I opened a bar for mustelids. My pop said it was a bad idea. First customer was a weasel who tried to pay with a bottle cap.
My German friend replaced his lightbulb. Now the room is 14% more logically illuminated.
A cow's favorite movie genre is the moo-stery, especially ones where they have to figure out who took the last of the milk.
A girl asked my friend to describe himself in three words. He said, "Not good at math." Her friend is still trying to figure it out.
I started a band called "999 Megabytes." We haven't gotten a gig yet.
What is a scientist's favorite dog breed? A laboratory retriever.
Anna's house doesn't have cracks in the foundation; her daughter, the drummer, calls them "fault lines of funk."
A centaur's biggest internal conflict is that his human half loves mayo, but his horse half always wants to neigh in protest.
"Agent Fin, your mission, should you choose to accept it," the snail read, "is to retrieve the algae wafer using this gun, without leaving the tank."
A lawyer fell off his bar stool. He immediately sued it for lack of support.
I tried to save money by packing my own parachute. It was a rip-off.
A 1960s Mod on a scooter is cool. A 1960s Mod in a minivan is just a dad.
My diet is very strict: no carbs, no sugar, and no using glue to attach tuna to my piano.
A pessimist sees the fall. An optimist sees the view. A skydiver sees the ground coming up way too fast.
What's a cow's favorite ballet move? The ground beef, where you just lie there and don't use a leg.
My friend told a girl he could read her palm. He said, "Your lifeline is very long," and she said, "Good, because this date feels like it will last an eternity."
A horse's idea of a horror movie is just a 90-minute, single-shot film of a spoon slowly being lowered into a jar of mayo, with a faint "neigh" in the background.
